content.text:
Base directory for this skill: /Users/vancityayush/.claude/plugins/cache/claude-plugins-official/superpowers/5.0.7/skills/subagent-driven-development

# Subagent-Driven Development

Execute plan by dispatching fresh subagent per task, with two-stage review after each: spec compliance review first, then code quality review.

**Why subagents:** You delegate tasks to specialized agents with isolated context. By precisely crafting their instructions and context, you ensure they stay focused and succeed at their task. They should never inherit your session's context or history — you construct exactly what they need. This also preserves your own context for coordination work.

**Core principle:** Fresh subagent per task + two-stage review (spec then quality) = high quality, fast iteration

## When to Use

```dot
digraph when_to_use {
    "Have implementation plan?" [shape=diamond];
    "Tasks mostly independent?" [shape=diamond];
    "Stay in this session?" [shape=diamond];
    "subagent-driven-development" [shape=box];
    "executing-plans" [shape=box];
    "Manual execution or brainstorm first" [shape=box];

    "Have implementation plan?" -> "Tasks mostly independent?" [label="yes"];
    "Have implementation plan?" -> "Manual execution or brainstorm first" [label="no"];
    "Tasks mostly independent?" -> "Stay in this session?" [label="yes"];
    "Tasks mostly independent?" -> "Manual execution or brainstorm first" [label="no - tightly coupled"];
    "Stay in this session?" -> "subagent-driven-development" [label="yes"];
    "Stay in this session?" -> "executing-plans" [label="no - parallel session"];
}
```

**vs. Executing Plans (parallel session):**
- Same session (no context switch)
- Fresh subagent per task (no context pollution)
- Two-stage review after each task: spec compliance first, then code quality
- Faster iteration (no human-in-loop between tasks)

## The Process

```dot
digraph process {
    rankdir=TB;

    subgraph cluster_per_task {
        label="Per Task";
        "Dispatch implementer subagent (./implementer-prompt.md)" [shape=box];
        "Implementer subagent asks questions?" [shape=diamond];
        "Answer questions, provide context" [shape=box];
        "Implementer subagent implements, tests, commits, self-reviews" [shape=box];
        "Dispatch spec reviewer subagent (./spec-reviewer-prompt.md)" [shape=box];
        "Spec reviewer subagent confirms code matches spec?" [shape=diamond];
        "Implementer subagent fixes spec gaps" [shape=box];
        "Dispatch code quality reviewer subagent (./code-quality-reviewer-prompt.md)" [shape=box];
        "Code quality reviewer subagent approves?" [shape=diamond];
        "Implementer subagent fixes quality issues" [shape=box];
        "Mark task complete in TodoWrite" [shape=box];
    }

    "Read plan, extract all tasks with full text, note context, create TodoWrite" [shape=box];
    "More tasks remain?" [shape=diamond];
    "Dispatch final code reviewer subagent for entire implementation" [shape=box];
    "Use superpowers:finishing-a-development-branch" [shape=box style=filled fillcolor=lightgreen];

    "Read plan, extract all tasks with full text, note context, create TodoWrite" -> "Dispatch implementer subagent (./implementer-prompt.md)";
    "Dispatch implementer subagent (./implementer-prompt.md)" -> "Implementer subagent asks questions?";
    "Implementer subagent asks questions?" -> "Answer questions, provide context" [label="yes"];
    "Answer questions, provide context" -> "Dispatch implementer subagent (./implementer-prompt.md)";
    "Implementer subagent asks questions?" -> "Implementer subagent implements, tests, commits, self-reviews" [label="no"];
    "Implementer subagent implements, tests, commits, self-reviews" -> "Dispatch spec reviewer subagent (./spec-reviewer-prompt.md)";
    "Dispatch spec reviewer subagent (./spec-reviewer-prompt.md)" -> "Spec reviewer subagent confirms code matches spec?";
    "Spec reviewer subagent confirms code matches spec?" -> "Implementer subagent fixes spec gaps" [label="no"];
    "Implementer subagent fixes spec gaps" -> "Dispatch spec reviewer subagent (./spec-reviewer-prompt.md)" [label="re-review"];
    "Spec reviewer subagent confirms code matches spec?" -> "Dispatch code quality reviewer subagent (./code-quality-reviewer-prompt.md)" [label="yes"];
    "Dispatch code quality reviewer subagent (./code-quality-reviewer-prompt.md)" -> "Code quality reviewer subagent approves?";
    "Code quality reviewer subagent approves?" -> "Implementer subagent fixes quality issues" [label="no"];
    "Implementer subagent fixes quality issues" -> "Dispatch code quality reviewer subagent (./code-quality-reviewer-prompt.md)" [label="re-review"];
    "Code quality reviewer subagent approves?" -> "Mark task complete in TodoWrite" [label="yes"];
    "Mark task complete in TodoWrite" -> "More tasks remain?";
    "More tasks remain?" -> "Dispatch implementer subagent (./implementer-prompt.md)" [label="yes"];
    "More tasks remain?" -> "Dispatch final code reviewer subagent for entire implementation" [label="no"];
    "Dispatch final code reviewer subagent for entire implementation" -> "Use superpowers:finishing-a-development-branch";
}
```

## Model Selection

Use the least powerful model that can handle each role to conserve cost and increase speed.

**Mechanical implementation tasks** (isolated functions, clear specs, 1-2 files): use a fast, cheap model. Most implementation tasks are mechanical when the plan is well-specified.

**Integration and judgment tasks** (multi-file coordination, pattern matching, debugging): use a standard model.

**Architecture, design, and review tasks**: use the most capable available model.

**Task complexity signals:**
- Touches 1-2 files with a complete spec → cheap model
- Touches multiple files with integration concerns → standard model
- Requires design judgment or broad codebase understanding → most capable model

## Handling Implementer Status

Implementer subagents report one of four statuses. Handle each appropriately:

**DONE:** Proceed to spec compliance review.

**DONE_WITH_CONCERNS:** The implementer completed the work but flagged doubts. Read the concerns before proceeding. If the concerns are about correctness or scope, address them before review. If they're observations (e.g., "this file is getting large"), note them and proceed to review.

**NEEDS_CONTEXT:** The implementer needs information that wasn't provided. Provide the missing context and re-dispatch.

**BLOCKED:** The implementer cannot complete the task. Assess the blocker:
1. If it's a context problem, provide more context and re-dispatch with the same model
2. If the task requires more reasoning, re-dispatch with a more capable model
3. If the task is too large, break it into smaller pieces
4. If the plan itself is wrong, escalate to the human

**Never** ignore an escalation or force the same model to retry without changes. If the implementer said it's stuck, something needs to change.

## Prompt Templates

- `./implementer-prompt.md` - Dispatch implementer subagent
- `./spec-reviewer-prompt.md` - Dispatch spec compliance reviewer subagent
- `./code-quality-reviewer-prompt.md` - Dispatch code quality reviewer subagent

## Example Workflow

```
You: I'm using Subagent-Driven Development to execute this plan.

[Read plan file once: docs/superpowers/plans/feature-plan.md]
[Extract all 5 tasks with full text and context]
[Create TodoWrite with all tasks]

Task 1: Hook installation script

[Get Task 1 text and context (already extracted)]
[Dispatch implementation subagent with full task text + context]

Implementer: "Before I begin - should the hook be installed at user or system level?"

You: "User level (~/.config/superpowers/hooks/)"

Implementer: "Got it. Implementing now..."
[Later] Implementer:
  - Implemented install-hook command
  - Added tests, 5/5 passing
  - Self-review: Found I missed --force flag, added it
  - Committed

[Dispatch spec compliance reviewer]
Spec reviewer: ✅ Spec compliant - all requirements met, nothing extra

[Get git SHAs, dispatch code quality reviewer]
Code reviewer: Strengths: Good test coverage, clean. Issues: None. Approved.

[Mark Task 1 complete]

Task 2: Recovery modes

[Get Task 2 text and context (already extracted)]
[Dispatch implementation subagent with full task text + context]

Implementer: [No questions, proceeds]
Implementer:
  - Added verify/repair modes
  - 8/8 tests passing
  - Self-review: All good
  - Committed

[Dispatch spec compliance reviewer]
Spec reviewer: ❌ Issues:
  - Missing: Progress reporting (spec says "report every 100 items")
  - Extra: Added --json flag (not requested)

[Implementer fixes issues]
Implementer: Removed --json flag, added progress reporting

[Spec reviewer reviews again]
Spec reviewer: ✅ Spec compliant now

[Dispatch code quality reviewer]
Code reviewer: Strengths: Solid. Issues (Important): Magic number (100)

[Implementer fixes]
Implementer: Extracted PROGRESS_INTERVAL constant

[Code reviewer reviews again]
Code reviewer: ✅ Approved

[Mark Task 2 complete]

...

[After all tasks]
[Dispatch final code-reviewer]
Final reviewer: All requirements met, ready to merge

Done!
```

## Advantages

**vs. Manual execution:**
- Subagents follow TDD naturally
- Fresh context per task (no confusion)
- Parallel-safe (subagents don't interfere)
- Subagent can ask questions (before AND during work)

**vs. Executing Plans:**
- Same session (no handoff)
- Continuous progress (no waiting)
- Review checkpoints automatic

**Efficiency gains:**
- No file reading overhead (controller provides full text)
- Controller curates exactly what context is needed
- Subagent gets complete information upfront
- Questions surfaced before work begins (not after)

**Quality gates:**
- Self-review catches issues before handoff
- Two-stage review: spec compliance, then code quality
- Review loops ensure fixes actually work
- Spec compliance prevents over/under-building
- Code quality ensures implementation is well-built

**Cost:**
- More subagent invocations (implementer + 2 reviewers per task)
- Controller does more prep work (extracting all tasks upfront)
- Review loops add iterations
- But catches issues early (cheaper than debugging later)

## Red Flags

**Never:**
- Start implementation on main/master branch without explicit user consent
- Skip reviews (spec compliance OR code quality)
- Proceed with unfixed issues
- Dispatch multiple implementation subagents in parallel (conflicts)
- Make subagent read plan file (provide full text instead)
- Skip scene-setting context (subagent needs to understand where task fits)
- Ignore subagent questions (answer before letting them proceed)
- Accept "close enough" on spec compliance (spec reviewer found issues = not done)
- Skip review loops (reviewer found issues = implementer fixes = review again)
- Let implementer self-review replace actual review (both are needed)
- **Start code quality review before spec compliance is ✅** (wrong order)
- Move to next task while either review has open issues

**If subagent asks questions:**
- Answer clearly and completely
- Provide additional context if needed
- Don't rush them into implementation

**If reviewer finds issues:**
- Implementer (same subagent) fixes them
- Reviewer reviews again
- Repeat until approved
- Don't skip the re-review

**If subagent fails task:**
- Dispatch fix subagent with specific instructions
- Don't try to fix manually (context pollution)

## Integration

**Required workflow skills:**
- **superpowers:using-git-worktrees** - REQUIRED: Set up isolated workspace before starting
- **superpowers:writing-plans** - Creates the plan this skill executes
- **superpowers:requesting-code-review** - Code review template for reviewer subagents
- **superpowers:finishing-a-development-branch** - Complete development after all tasks

**Subagents should use:**
- **superpowers:test-driven-development** - Subagents follow TDD for each task

**Alternative workflow:**
- **superpowers:executing-plans** - Use for parallel session instead of same-session execution

content.text:
Base directory for this skill: /Users/vancityayush/.claude/plugins/cache/claude-plugins-official/superpowers/5.0.7/skills/using-git-worktrees

# Using Git Worktrees

## Overview

Git worktrees create isolated workspaces sharing the same repository, allowing work on multiple branches simultaneously without switching.

**Core principle:** Systematic directory selection + safety verification = reliable isolation.

**Announce at start:** "I'm using the using-git-worktrees skill to set up an isolated workspace."

## Directory Selection Process

Follow this priority order:

### 1. Check Existing Directories

```bash
# Check in priority order
ls -d .worktrees 2>/dev/null     # Preferred (hidden)
ls -d worktrees 2>/dev/null      # Alternative
```

**If found:** Use that directory. If both exist, `.worktrees` wins.

### 2. Check CLAUDE.md

```bash
grep -i "worktree.*director" CLAUDE.md 2>/dev/null
```

**If preference specified:** Use it without asking.

### 3. Ask User

If no directory exists and no CLAUDE.md preference:

```
No worktree directory found. Where should I create worktrees?

1. .worktrees/ (project-local, hidden)
2. ~/.config/superpowers/worktrees/<project-name>/ (global location)

Which would you prefer?
```

## Safety Verification

### For Project-Local Directories (.worktrees or worktrees)

**MUST verify directory is ignored before creating worktree:**

```bash
# Check if directory is ignored (respects local, global, and system gitignore)
git check-ignore -q .worktrees 2>/dev/null || git check-ignore -q worktrees 2>/dev/null
```

**If NOT ignored:**

Per Jesse's rule "Fix broken things immediately":
1. Add appropriate line to .gitignore
2. Commit the change
3. Proceed with worktree creation

**Why critical:** Prevents accidentally committing worktree contents to repository.

### For Global Directory (~/.config/superpowers/worktrees)

No .gitignore verification needed - outside project entirely.

## Creation Steps

### 1. Detect Project Name

```bash
project=$(basename "$(git rev-parse --show-toplevel)")
```

### 2. Create Worktree

```bash
# Determine full path
case $LOCATION in
  .worktrees|worktrees)
    path="$LOCATION/$BRANCH_NAME"
    ;;
  ~/.config/superpowers/worktrees/*)
    path="~/.config/superpowers/worktrees/$project/$BRANCH_NAME"
    ;;
esac

# Create worktree with new branch
git worktree add "$path" -b "$BRANCH_NAME"
cd "$path"
```

### 3. Run Project Setup

Auto-detect and run appropriate setup:

```bash
# Node.js
if [ -f package.json ]; then npm install; fi

# Rust
if [ -f Cargo.toml ]; then cargo build; fi

# Python
if [ -f requirements.txt ]; then pip install -r requirements.txt; fi
if [ -f pyproject.toml ]; then poetry install; fi

# Go
if [ -f go.mod ]; then go mod download; fi
```

### 4. Verify Clean Baseline

Run tests to ensure worktree starts clean:

```bash
# Examples - use project-appropriate command
npm test
cargo test
pytest
go test ./...
```

**If tests fail:** Report failures, ask whether to proceed or investigate.

**If tests pass:** Report ready.

### 5. Report Location

```
Worktree ready at <full-path>
Tests passing (<N> tests, 0 failures)
Ready to implement <feature-name>
```

## Quick Reference

| Situation | Action |
|-----------|--------|
| `.worktrees/` exists | Use it (verify ignored) |
| `worktrees/` exists | Use it (verify ignored) |
| Both exist | Use `.worktrees/` |
| Neither exists | Check CLAUDE.md → Ask user |
| Directory not ignored | Add to .gitignore + commit |
| Tests fail during baseline | Report failures + ask |
| No package.json/Cargo.toml | Skip dependency install |

## Common Mistakes

### Skipping ignore verification

- **Problem:** Worktree contents get tracked, pollute git status
- **Fix:** Always use `git check-ignore` before creating project-local worktree

### Assuming directory location

- **Problem:** Creates inconsistency, violates project conventions
- **Fix:** Follow priority: existing > CLAUDE.md > ask

### Proceeding with failing tests

- **Problem:** Can't distinguish new bugs from pre-existing issues
- **Fix:** Report failures, get explicit permission to proceed

### Hardcoding setup commands

- **Problem:** Breaks on projects using different tools
- **Fix:** Auto-detect from project files (package.json, etc.)

## Example Workflow

```
You: I'm using the using-git-worktrees skill to set up an isolated workspace.

[Check .worktrees/ - exists]
[Verify ignored - git check-ignore confirms .worktrees/ is ignored]
[Create worktree: git worktree add .worktrees/auth -b feature/auth]
[Run npm install]
[Run npm test - 47 passing]

Worktree ready at /Users/jesse/myproject/.worktrees/auth
Tests passing (47 tests, 0 failures)
Ready to implement auth feature
```

## Red Flags

**Never:**
- Create worktree without verifying it's ignored (project-local)
- Skip baseline test verification
- Proceed with failing tests without asking
- Assume directory location when ambiguous
- Skip CLAUDE.md check

**Always:**
- Follow directory priority: existing > CLAUDE.md > ask
- Verify directory is ignored for project-local
- Auto-detect and run project setup
- Verify clean test baseline

## Integration

**Called by:**
- **brainstorming** (Phase 4) - REQUIRED when design is approved and implementation follows
- **subagent-driven-development** - REQUIRED before executing any tasks
- **executing-plans** - REQUIRED before executing any tasks
- Any skill needing isolated workspace

**Pairs with:**
- **finishing-a-development-branch** - REQUIRED for cleanup after work complete

# sshx TUI Rebuild — Implementation Plan

> **For agentic workers:** REQUIRED SUB-SKILL: Use superpowers:subagent-driven-development (recommended) or superpowers:executing-plans to implement this plan task-by-task. Steps use checkbox (`- [ ]`) syntax for tracking.

**Goal:** Rebuild sshx from scratch as a hybrid TUI/flag CLI using `@vancityayush/tui`, with pure command modules and a React screen router.

**Architecture:** Command functions in `src/commands/` are pure async (no UI deps). TUI in `src/tui/` calls them. `src/cli.ts` routes: known command + args → headless, no args → `render(<App />)`. Managed SSH config blocks carry a `# sshx` marker for tracking.

**Tech Stack:** TypeScript + React JSX, `@vancityayush/tui`, Bun runtime, `bun test` for unit tests, `bun build` for bundling.

---

## File Map

| File | Responsibility |
|------|---------------|
| `src/providers.ts` | `ProviderKey` type + `PROVIDERS` record |
| `src/platform.ts` | OS detection, clipboard copy, browser open |
| `src/ssh/config.ts` | Parse/serialize/read/write `~/.ssh/config`; managed-marker detection |
| `src/ssh/config.test.ts` | Unit tests for config parsing (pure functions) |
| `src/ssh/keygen.ts` | Shell out to `ssh-keygen`, read pubkey |
| `src/commands/list.ts` | Return `ManagedKey[]` from SSH config |
| `src/commands/setup.ts` | Generate key + write config + agent + clipboard + browser |
| `src/commands/remove.ts` | Delete key files + remove config entry + agent |
| `src/commands/test.ts` | Run `ssh -T` and return result |
| `src/commands/agent.ts` | List/add/remove keys in ssh-agent |
| `src/commands/copy.ts` | Copy a managed key's pubkey to clipboard |
| `src/tui/components/Header.tsx` | Shared title bar |
| `src/tui/screens/MainMenu.tsx` | `CustomSelect` main menu |
| `src/tui/screens/Setup.tsx` | 4-step wizard: provider → email → key name → generate |
| `src/tui/screens/KeyList.tsx` | List with copy/remove per key |
| `src/tui/screens/TestConnection.tsx` | Provider select + run `ssh -T` |
| `src/tui/screens/Agent.tsx` | Show agent keys + add/remove |
| `src/tui/App.tsx` | `currentScreen` state router |
| `src/cli.ts` | Entry point: arg parsing → headless or TUI |

---

## Task 1: Scaffold — clean slate, deps, config

**Files:**
- Modify: `package.json`
- Modify: `tsconfig.json`
- Delete: `src/cli.ts` (replaced in Task 20)

- [ ] **Step 1: Delete all existing src files**

```bash
rm -rf src/
mkdir -p src/commands src/ssh src/tui/screens src/tui/components
```

- [ ] **Step 2: Install dependencies**

```bash
# If @vancityayush/tui is published to npm:
bun add @vancityayush/tui react

# If not yet published — clone it, build it, then:
# bun add file:/path/to/tui react

bun add -d @types/react
```

- [ ] **Step 3: Update `package.json`** — add `react` to deps, update scripts

186	    label: "Bitbucket",
187	    hostname: "bitbucket.org",
188	    settingsUrl: "https://bitbucket.org/account/settings/ssh-keys/",
189	    keyType: "ed25519",
190	  },
191	  azure: {
192	    label: "Azure DevOps",
193	    hostname: "ssh.dev.azure.com",
194	    settingsUrl: "https://dev.azure.com/_usersSettings/keys",
195	    keyType: "rsa",
196	  },
197	}

199	export const PROVIDER_KEYS = Object.keys(PROVIDERS) as ProviderKey[]
200	
201	export function detectProviderFromHostname(hostname: string): ProviderKey | undefined {
202	  for (const [key, provider] of Object.entries(PROVIDERS) as [ProviderKey, Provider][]) {
203	    if (hostname.includes(provider.hostname)) return key
204	  }
205	  return undefined
206	}
207	
208	export function detectProviderFromRemoteUrl(url: string): ProviderKey | undefined {
209	  if (url.includes("github.com")) return "github"
210	  if (url.includes("gitlab.com")) return "gitlab"
211	  if (url.includes("bitbucket.org")) return "bitbucket"
212	  if (url.includes("dev.azure.com") || url.includes("vs-ssh.visualstudio.com")) return "azure"
213	  return undefined
214	}
215	
216	export function normalizeProviderKey(value: string): ProviderKey | undefined {
217	  const v = value.trim().toLowerCase()
218	  if (v === "github" || v === "gh") return "github"
219	  if (v === "gitlab" || v === "gl") return "gitlab"
220	  if (v === "bitbucket" || v === "bb") return "bitbucket"
221	  if (v === "azure" || v === "azure-devops" || v === "azuredevops") return "azure"
222	  return undefined

243	## Task 3: `src/platform.ts` — OS detection, clipboard, browser
244	
245	**Files:**
246	- Create: `src/platform.ts`
247	
248	- [ ] **Step 1: Write the file**
249	
250	```typescript
251	import { existsSync, readFileSync } from "node:fs"
252	import { spawnSync } from "node:child_process"
253	
254	export type Platform = "macos" | "linux" | "windows" | "wsl"
255	
256	export function detectPlatform(): Platform {
257	  if (process.platform === "win32") return "windows"
258	  if (process.platform === "darwin") return "macos"
259	  try {
260	    const v = readFileSync("/proc/version", "utf8")
261	    if (v.includes("Microsoft")) return "wsl"
262	  } catch {}
263	  return "linux"
264	}
265	
266	function commandExists(cmd: string): boolean {
267	  const lookup = process.platform === "win32" ? "where" : "which"
268	  return spawnSync(lookup, [cmd], { stdio: "ignore" }).status === 0
269	}
270	
271	function tryCopy(text: string, command: string, args: string[] = []): boolean {
272	  if (!commandExists(command)) return false
273	  return spawnSync(command, args, { input: text, encoding: "utf8" }).status === 0
274	}
275	
276	export function copyToClipboard(text: string): boolean {
277	  const platform = detectPlatform()
278	  if (platform === "macos") return tryCopy(text, "pbcopy")
279	  if (platform === "windows") return tryCopy(text, "clip.exe") || tryCopy(text, "clip")
280	  if (platform === "wsl") {
281	    return tryCopy(text, "clip.exe") ||
282	      tryCopy(text, "xclip", ["-selection", "clipboard"]) ||
283	      tryCopy(text, "xsel", ["--clipboard", "--input"])
284	  }
285	  return tryCopy(text, "xclip", ["-selection", "clipboard"]) ||
286	    tryCopy(text, "xsel", ["--clipboard", "--input"])
287	}
288	
289	function tryOpen(command: string, args: string[]): boolean {
290	  if (!commandExists(command)) return false
291	  return spawnSync(command, args, { stdio: "ignore" }).status === 0
292	}
293	
294	export function openInBrowser(url: string): boolean {
295	  const platform = detectPlatform()
296	  if (platform === "macos") return tryOpen("open", [url])
297	  if (platform === "windows") {
298	    return tryOpen("cmd", ["/c", "start", "", url]) ||
299	      tryOpen("powershell", ["-NoProfile", "-Command", "Start-Process", url])
300	  }
301	  return tryOpen("xdg-open", [url])
302	}
303	```

322	## Task 4: `src/ssh/config.ts` — SSH config parser/writer
323	
324	**Files:**
325	- Create: `src/ssh/config.ts`
326	
327	- [ ] **Step 1: Write the file**
328	
329	```typescript
330	import { existsSync, mkdirSync, readFileSync, writeFileSync } from "node:fs"
331	import { homedir } from "node:os"
332	import { join, normalize } from "node:path"
333	import { type ProviderKey, PROVIDERS, detectProviderFromHostname } from "../providers.js"
334	
335	export const SSH_DIR = join(homedir(), ".ssh")
336	export const SSH_CONFIG_PATH = join(SSH_DIR, "config")
337	const SSHX_MARKER = "# sshx"
338	
339	export type ConfigSegment =
340	  | { kind: "text"; lines: string[] }
341	  | { kind: "host"; host: string; lines: string[]; managed: boolean }
342	
343	export function parseConfigSegments(content: string): ConfigSegment[] {
344	  const normalized = content.replace(/\r\n/g, "\n")
345	  if (!normalized.trim()) return []
346	
347	  const lines = normalized.split("\n")
348	  const segments: ConfigSegment[] = []
349	  let textLines: string[] = []
350	  let hostLines: string[] | null = null
351	  let hostName = ""
352	  let hostManaged = false
353	
354	  const flushText = () => {
355	    if (textLines.length > 0) {
356	      segments.push({ kind: "text", lines: [...textLines] })
357	      textLines = []
358	    }
359	  }
360	  const flushHost = () => {
361	    if (hostLines !== null) {
362	      segments.push({ kind: "host", host: hostName, lines: [...hostLines], managed: hostManaged })
363	      hostLines = null
364	    }
365	  }
366	
367	  for (const line of lines) {
368	    const isHostStart = /^Host\s+/.test(line) && !/^\s/.test(line)
369	    if (isHostStart) {
370	      flushText()
371	      flushHost()
372	      hostName = line.replace(/^Host\s+/, "").replace(/\s*#.*$/, "").trim()
373	      hostManaged = line.includes(SSHX_MARKER)
374	      hostLines = [line]
375	      continue
376	    }
377	    if (hostLines !== null) {
378	      hostLines.push(line)
379	    } else {
380	      textLines.push(line)
381	    }
382	  }
383	  flushText()
384	  flushHost()
385	  return segments
386	}
387	
388	export function serializeConfigSegments(segments: ConfigSegment[]): string {
389	  const blocks = segments
390	    .map(s => s.lines.join("\n").trimEnd())
391	    .filter(b => b.length > 0)
392	  if (blocks.length === 0) return ""
393	  return `${blocks.join("\n\n")}\n`
394	}
395	
396	export function readConfigSegments(): ConfigSegment[] {
397	  if (!existsSync(SSH_CONFIG_PATH)) return []
398	  return parseConfigSegments(readFileSync(SSH_CONFIG_PATH, "utf8"))
399	}
400	
401	export function writeConfigSegments(segments: ConfigSegment[]): void {
402	  mkdirSync(SSH_DIR, { recursive: true })
403	  writeFileSync(SSH_CONFIG_PATH, serializeConfigSegments(segments))
404	}
405	
406	export function extractIdentityFiles(lines: string[]): string[] {
407	  return lines
408	    .map(l => l.match(/^\s*IdentityFile\s+(.+)$/)?.[1]?.trim())
409	    .filter((v): v is string => Boolean(v))
410	    .map(v => v.replace(/^"(.*)"$/, "$1"))
411	}
412	
413	export function extractHostName(lines: string[]): string {
414	  const match = lines
415	    .map(l => l.match(/^\s*HostName\s+(.+)$/))
416	    .find(Boolean)
417	  return match?.[1]?.trim() ?? ""
418	}
419	
420	export function expandHomePath(p: string): string {
421	  if (p === "~") return homedir()
422	  if (p.startsWith("~/")) return join(homedir(), p.slice(2))
423	  return p
424	}
425	
426	export function normalizePath(p: string): string {
427	  return normalize(expandHomePath(p)).replace(/\\/g, "/")
428	}
429	
430	export function buildHostBlock(
431	  provider: ProviderKey,
432	  host: string,
433	  keyName: string,
434	): string[] {
435	  const lines = [
436	    `Host ${host}  ${SSHX_MARKER}`,
437	    `  HostName ${PROVIDERS[provider].hostname}`,
438	    `  User git`,
439	    `  AddKeysToAgent yes`,
440	    `  IdentityFile ~/.ssh/${keyName}`,
441	    `  IdentitiesOnly yes`,
442	  ]
443	  if (provider === "azure") {
444	    lines.push("  HostkeyAlgorithms +ssh-rsa")
445	    lines.push("  PubkeyAcceptedAlgorithms +ssh-rsa")
446	  }
447	  return lines
448	}
449	
450	export function upsertHostBlock(
451	  provider: ProviderKey,
452	  host: string,
453	  keyName: string,
454	): "added" | "replaced" {
455	  const segments = readConfigSegments()
456	  const block: ConfigSegment = {
457	    kind: "host",
458	    host,
459	    lines: buildHostBlock(provider, host, keyName),
460	    managed: true,
461	  }
462	  let replaced = false
463	  const next = segments.map(s => {
464	    if (s.kind === "host" && s.host === host) {
465	      replaced = true
466	      return block
467	    }
468	    return s
469	  })
470	  if (!replaced) next.push(block)
471	  writeConfigSegments(next)
472	  return replaced ? "replaced" : "added"
473	}
474	
475	export function removeManagedHostBlocksForKey(keyName: string): string[] {
476	  const variants = new Set([
477	    normalizePath(join(SSH_DIR, keyName)),
478	    normalizePath(`~/.ssh/${keyName}`),
479	    normalizePath(keyName),
480	  ])
481	  const removed: string[] = []
482	  const next = readConfigSegments().filter(s => {
483	    if (s.kind !== "host" || !s.managed) return true
484	    const matched = extractIdentityFiles(s.lines)
485	      .some(f => variants.has(normalizePath(f)))
486	    if (matched) {
487	      removed.push(s.host)
488	      return false
489	    }
490	    return true
491	  })
492	  writeConfigSegments(next)
493	  return removed
494	}
495	
496	export function listManagedHostBlocks(): Array<{
497	  host: string
498	  identityFiles: string[]
499	  hostname: string
500	}> {
501	  return readConfigSegments()
502	    .filter((s): s is Extract<ConfigSegment, { kind: "host" }> =>
503	      s.kind === "host" && s.managed)
504	    .map(s => ({
505	      host: s.host,
506	      identityFiles: extractIdentityFiles(s.lines),
507	      hostname: extractHostName(s.lines),
508	    }))
509	}
510	```

812	## Task 7: `src/commands/list.ts` — list managed keys
813	
814	**Files:**
815	- Create: `src/commands/list.ts`
816	
817	- [ ] **Step 1: Write the file**
818	
819	```typescript
820	import { existsSync } from "node:fs"
821	import { join } from "node:path"
822	import { type ProviderKey } from "../providers.js"
823	import {
824	  listManagedHostBlocks,
825	  SSH_DIR,
826	  expandHomePath,
827	  extractIdentityFiles,
828	} from "../ssh/config.js"
829	import { detectProviderFromHostname } from "../providers.js"
830	
831	export type ManagedKey = {
832	  name: string
833	  host: string
834	  provider?: ProviderKey
835	  publicKeyPath: string
836	}
837	
838	export async function list(): Promise<ManagedKey[]> {
839	  const blocks = listManagedHostBlocks()
840	  return blocks.map(block => {
841	    const identityFiles = block.identityFiles
842	    const raw = identityFiles[0] ?? ""
843	    const keyName = raw.replace(/^.*[\\/]/, "")
844	    const expanded = expandHomePath(raw)
845	    const publicKeyPath = `${expanded}.pub`
846	    return {
847	      name: keyName,
848	      host: block.host,
849	      provider: detectProviderFromHostname(block.hostname),
850	      publicKeyPath,
851	    }
852	  })
853	}

874	
875	**Files:**

877	
878	- [ ] **Step 1: Write the file**
879	
880	```typescript
881	import { join } from "node:path"
882	import { writeFileSync, unlinkSync, existsSync } from "node:fs"
883	import { spawnSync } from "node:child_process"
884	import { homedir } from "node:os"
885	import { type ProviderKey, PROVIDERS } from "../providers.js"
886	import {
887	  upsertHostBlock,
888	  SSH_DIR,
889	  removeManagedHostBlocksForKey,
890	} from "../ssh/config.js"
891	import { generateKey, readPublicKey, keyExists, deleteKeyFiles } from "../ssh/keygen.js"
892	import { copyToClipboard, openInBrowser, detectPlatform } from "../platform.js"
893	
894	export type SetupOptions = {
895	  provider: ProviderKey
896	  email: string
897	  keyName: string
898	  host?: string
899	  force?: boolean
900	  noGitConfig?: boolean
901	  noBrowser?: boolean
902	  noClipboard?: boolean
903	}
904	
905	export type SetupResult = {
906	  keyName: string
907	  host: string
908	  publicKey: string
909	  clipboardCopied: boolean
910	  browserOpened: boolean
911	}
912	
913	export async function setup(opts: SetupOptions): Promise<SetupResult> {
914	  const host = opts.host ?? PROVIDERS[opts.provider].hostname
915	  const keyName = opts.keyName
916	
917	  if (keyExists(keyName) && !opts.force) {
918	    throw new Error(
919	      `Key "${keyName}" already exists in ~/.ssh. Use --force to overwrite.`
920	    )
921	  }
922	
923	  // Generate key files
924	  generateKey(opts.provider, opts.email, keyName)
925	
926	  // Write SSH config — rollback key files if this fails
927	  try {
928	    upsertHostBlock(opts.provider, host, keyName)
929	  } catch (err) {
930	    deleteKeyFiles(keyName)
931	    throw err
932	  }
933	
934	  // Add to ssh-agent (non-fatal)
935	  addToAgent(keyName)
936	
937	  // Configure per-host git identity (non-fatal, opt-out with noGitConfig)
938	  if (!opts.noGitConfig) {
939	    try {
940	      configureGitForKey(keyName, host, opts.email)
941	    } catch {}
942	  }
943	
944	  const publicKey = readPublicKey(keyName)
945	
946	  // Copy to clipboard (non-fatal)
947	  const clipboardCopied = !opts.noClipboard && copyToClipboard(publicKey)
948	
949	  // Open browser (non-fatal)
950	  const browserOpened = !opts.noBrowser && openInBrowser(PROVIDERS[opts.provider].settingsUrl)
951	
952	  return { keyName, host, publicKey, clipboardCopied, browserOpened }
953	}
954	
955	function addToAgent(keyName: string): void {
956	  const keyPath = join(SSH_DIR, keyName)
957	  if (spawnSync("which", ["ssh-add"], { stdio: "ignore" }).status !== 0) return
958	  ensureAgent()
959	  const platform = detectPlatform()
960	  const args = platform === "macos" ? ["--apple-use-keychain", keyPath] : [keyPath]
961	  const r = spawnSync("ssh-add", args, { encoding: "utf8" })
962	  if (r.status !== 0) {
963	    spawnSync("ssh-add", [keyPath], { encoding: "utf8" })
964	  }
965	}
966	
967	function ensureAgent(): void {
968	  if (detectPlatform() === "windows") return
969	  if (process.env.SSH_AUTH_SOCK) return
970	  const r = spawnSync("ssh-agent", ["-s"], { encoding: "utf8" })
971	  if (r.status !== 0) return
972	  for (const match of (r.stdout ?? "").matchAll(/(\w+)=([^;]+);/g)) {
973	    process.env[match[1]] = match[2]
974	  }
975	}
976	
977	function configureGitForKey(keyName: string, host: string, email: string): void {
978	  if (spawnSync("which", ["git"], { stdio: "ignore" }).status !== 0) return
979	  const gitConfigPath = join(SSH_DIR, `.gitconfig-${keyName}`)
980	  writeFileSync(gitConfigPath, `[user]\n    email = ${email}\n`)
981	  const keys = [
982	    `includeIf."hasconfig:remote.*.url:git@${host}:*/**".path`,
983	    `includeIf."hasconfig:remote.*.url:ssh://git@${host}/**".path`,
984	  ]
985	  for (const key of keys) {
986	    spawnSync("git", ["config", "--global", key, gitConfigPath], { encoding: "utf8" })
987	  }
988	}
989	
990	export function readGitContext(): { email?: string; remoteUrl?: string } {
991	  if (spawnSync("which", ["git"], { stdio: "ignore" }).status !== 0) return {}
992	  const email = spawnSync("git", ["config", "--global", "user.email"], { encoding: "utf8" })
993	  const remote = spawnSync("git", ["remote", "get-url", "origin"], { encoding: "utf8" })
994	  return {
995	    email: email.status === 0 ? email.stdout.trim() : undefined,
996	    remoteUrl: remote.status === 0 ? remote.stdout.trim() : undefined,
997	  }
998	}
999	```

1025	```typescript
1026	import { join } from "node:path"
1027	import { existsSync, unlinkSync } from "node:fs"
1028	import { spawnSync } from "node:child_process"
1029	import { SSH_DIR, removeManagedHostBlocksForKey } from "../ssh/config.js"
1030	import { deleteKeyFiles } from "../ssh/keygen.js"
1031	
1032	export async function remove(keyName: string): Promise<{ removedHosts: string[] }> {
1033	  const keyPath = join(SSH_DIR, keyName)
1034	
1035	  // Remove from agent (non-fatal)
1036	  removeFromAgent(keyPath)
1037	
1038	  // Remove managed SSH config entries
1039	  const removedHosts = removeManagedHostBlocksForKey(keyName)
1040	
1041	  // Delete key files
1042	  deleteKeyFiles(keyName)
1043	
1044	  // Remove per-host git config (non-fatal)
1045	  removeGitConfig(keyName)
1046	
1047	  return { removedHosts }
1048	}
1049	
1050	function removeFromAgent(keyPath: string): void {
1051	  if (spawnSync("which", ["ssh-add"], { stdio: "ignore" }).status !== 0) return
1052	  spawnSync("ssh-add", ["-d", keyPath], { encoding: "utf8" })
1053	}
1054	
1055	function removeGitConfig(keyName: string): void {
1056	  const gitConfigPath = join(SSH_DIR, `.gitconfig-${keyName}`)
1057	  try { unlinkSync(gitConfigPath) } catch {}
1058	  if (spawnSync("which", ["git"], { stdio: "ignore" }).status !== 0) return
1059	  const result = spawnSync(
1060	    "git",
1061	    ["config", "--global", "--get-regexp", "^includeIf\\..*\\.path$"],
1062	    { encoding: "utf8" }
1063	  )
1064	  if (result.status !== 0) return
1065	  const normalizedTarget = gitConfigPath.replace(/\\/g, "/")
1066	  result.stdout
1067	    .split("\n")
1068	    .filter(Boolean)
1069	    .forEach(line => {
1070	      const sep = line.indexOf(" ")
1071	      if (sep === -1) return
1072	      const key = line.slice(0, sep)
1073	      const val = line.slice(sep + 1).trim().replace(/\\/g, "/")
1074	      if (val === normalizedTarget) {
1075	        spawnSync("git", ["config", "--global", "--unset", key], { encoding: "utf8" })
1076	      }
1077	    })
1078	}
1079	```

1616	```tsx
1617	import React, { useState, useEffect } from "react"
1618	import { Box, Text, Dialog, ListItem, useInput, Spinner } from "@vancityayush/tui"
1619	import { Header } from "../components/Header.js"
1620	import { list, type ManagedKey } from "../../commands/list.js"
1621	import { remove } from "../../commands/remove.js"
1622	import { copy } from "../../commands/copy.js"
1623	import { PROVIDERS } from "../../providers.js"
1624	
1625	type Props = {
1626	  onBack: () => void
1627	}
1628	
1629	export function KeyList({ onBack }: Props): React.ReactNode {
1630	  const [keys, setKeys] = useState<ManagedKey[]>([])
1631	  const [loading, setLoading] = useState(true)
1632	  const [selectedIndex, setSelectedIndex] = useState(0)
1633	  const [confirmRemove, setConfirmRemove] = useState<string | null>(null)
1634	  const [status, setStatus] = useState<string | null>(null)
1635	  const [dialogActive, setDialogActive] = useState(false)
1636	
1637	  useEffect(() => {
1638	    list().then(k => {
1639	      setKeys(k)
1640	      setLoading(false)
1641	    })
1642	  }, [])
1643	
1644	  useInput((_input, key) => {
1645	    if (key.escape) {
1646	      onBack()
1647	      return
1648	    }
1649	    if (key.upArrow) setSelectedIndex(i => Math.max(0, i - 1))
1650	    if (key.downArrow) setSelectedIndex(i => Math.min(keys.length - 1, i + 1))
1651	    if (key.return && keys[selectedIndex]) {
1652	      handleCopy(keys[selectedIndex]!.name)
1653	    }
1654	    if (key.delete || (_input === "d" && !key.ctrl)) {
1655	      if (keys[selectedIndex]) {
1656	        setConfirmRemove(keys[selectedIndex]!.name)
1657	        setDialogActive(true)
1658	      }
1659	    }
1660	  }, { isActive: !dialogActive && !loading })
1661	
1662	  useInput((_input, key) => {
1663	    if (key.escape) {
1664	      setConfirmRemove(null)
1665	      setDialogActive(false)
1666	    }
1667	    if (key.return && confirmRemove) {
1668	      handleRemove(confirmRemove)
1669	    }
1670	  }, { isActive: dialogActive })
1671	
1672	  async function handleCopy(name: string): Promise<void> {
1673	    try {
1674	      const r = await copy(name)
1675	      setStatus(r.copied ? `Copied ${name} public key to clipboard` : `Could not copy — key: ${r.publicKey.slice(0, 40)}…`)
1676	    } catch (err) {
1677	      setStatus(err instanceof Error ? err.message : String(err))
1678	    }
1679	  }
1680	
1681	  async function handleRemove(name: string): Promise<void> {
1682	    setConfirmRemove(null)
1683	    setDialogActive(false)
1684	    try {
1685	      await remove(name)
1686	      const updated = await list()
1687	      setKeys(updated)
1688	      setSelectedIndex(i => Math.min(i, updated.length - 1))
1689	      setStatus(`Removed ${name}`)
1690	    } catch (err) {
1691	      setStatus(err instanceof Error ? err.message : String(err))
1692	    }
1693	  }
1694	
1695	  if (loading) {
1696	    return (
1697	      <Box flexDirection="column">
1698	        <Header subtitle="Managed SSH keys" />
1699	        <Spinner label="Loading keys…" />
1700	      </Box>
1701	    )
1702	  }
1703	
1704	  return (
1705	    <Box flexDirection="column">
1706	      <Header subtitle={`${keys.length} managed key${keys.length !== 1 ? "s" : ""}`} />
1707	
1708	      {keys.length === 0 && (
1709	        <Text dimColor>No managed keys found. Run setup to create one.</Text>
1710	      )}
1711	
1712	      {keys.map((k, i) => (
1713	        <ListItem
1714	          key={k.name}
1715	          isFocused={i === selectedIndex}
1716	          isSelected={i === selectedIndex}
1717	          description={`${k.host} · ${k.provider ? PROVIDERS[k.provider].label : "unknown provider"}`}
1718	        >
1719	          {k.name}
1720	        </ListItem>
1721	      ))}
1722	
1723	      {status && (
1724	        <Box marginTop={1}>
1725	          <Text dimColor>{status}</Text>
1726	        </Box>
1727	      )}
1728	
1729	      <Box marginTop={1}>
1730	        <Text dimColor>↑↓ navigate · Enter copy pubkey · d remove · Esc back</Text>
1731	      </Box>
1732	
1733	      {confirmRemove && (
1734	        <Dialog
1735	          title={`Remove "${confirmRemove}"?`}
1736	          subtitle="This will delete the key files and SSH config entry."
1737	          onCancel={() => {
1738	            setConfirmRemove(null)
1739	            setDialogActive(false)
1740	          }}
1741	        >
1742	          <Text>Press Enter to confirm or Esc to cancel.</Text>
1743	        </Dialog>
1744	      )}
1745	    </Box>
1746	  )
1747	}
1748	```

1902	import React, { useState, useEffect } from "react"
1903	import { Box, Text, ListItem, CustomSelect, Spinner, useInput } from "@vancityayush/tui"
1904	import { Header } from "../components/Header.js"

1906	import { list } from "../../commands/list.js"
1907	
1908	type Props = {
1909	  onBack: () => void
1910	}
1911	
1912	type View = "list" | "add-select"
1913	
1914	export function Agent({ onBack }: Props): React.ReactNode {
1915	  const [view, setView] = useState<View>("list")
1916	  const [agentKeys, setAgentKeys] = useState<AgentKey[]>([])
1917	  const [managedKeys, setManagedKeys] = useState<{ label: string; value: string; description: string }[]>([])
1918	  const [loading, setLoading] = useState(true)
1919	  const [selectedIndex, setSelectedIndex] = useState(0)
1920	  const [status, setStatus] = useState<string | null>(null)
1921	  const [error, setError] = useState<string | null>(null)
1922	
1923	  async function refreshAgentKeys(): Promise<void> {
1924	    try {
1925	      const keys = await agentList()
1926	      setAgentKeys(keys)
1927	    } catch (err) {
1928	      setError(err instanceof Error ? err.message : String(err))
1929	    }
1930	  }
1931	
1932	  useEffect(() => {
1933	    Promise.all([
1934	      agentList().catch(() => [] as AgentKey[]),
1935	      list().catch(() => []),
1936	    ]).then(([aKeys, mKeys]) => {
1937	      setAgentKeys(aKeys)
1938	      setManagedKeys(mKeys.map(k => ({
1939	        label: k.name,
1940	        value: k.name,
1941	        description: k.host,
1942	      })))
1943	      setLoading(false)
1944	    })
1945	  }, [])
1946	
1947	  useInput((_input, key) => {
1948	    if (key.escape) {
1949	      if (view === "add-select") {
1950	        setView("list")
1951	      } else {
1952	        onBack()
1953	      }
1954	      return
1955	    }
1956	    if (view === "list") {
1957	      if (key.upArrow) setSelectedIndex(i => Math.max(0, i - 1))
1958	      if (key.downArrow) setSelectedIndex(i => Math.min(agentKeys.length - 1, i + 1))
1959	      if (_input === "a") setView("add-select")
1960	      if ((_input === "d" || key.delete) && agentKeys[selectedIndex]) {
1961	        handleRemove(agentKeys[selectedIndex]!.path)
1962	      }
1963	    }
1964	  }, { isActive: !loading })
1965

1968	    try {

1970	      setStatus(`Added ${keyName} to agent`)

1972	    } catch (err) {
1973	      setError(err instanceof Error ? err.message : String(err))
1974	    }
1975	  }
1976	
1977	  async function handleRemove(path: string): Promise<void> {
1978	    const name = path.split("/").pop() ?? path
1979	    try {

1982	      await refreshAgentKeys()
1983	    } catch (err) {

1985	    }
1986	  }
1987	
1988	  if (loading) {

1994	    )

2015	        />
2016	        <Box marginTop={1}><Text dimColor>Esc to go back</Text></Box>
2017	      </Box>
2018	    )

2020	
2021	  return (

2023	      <Header subtitle={`SSH Agent — ${agentKeys.length} key${agentKeys.length !== 1 ? "s" : ""} loaded`} />
2024	
2025	      {agentKeys.length === 0 && (

2027	      )}
2028	
2029	      {agentKeys.map((k, i) => (
2030	        <ListItem
2031	          key={k.fingerprint}
2032	          isFocused={i === selectedIndex}
2033	          isSelected={i === selectedIndex}
2034	          description={k.type}
2035	        >
2036	          {k.path.split("/").pop() ?? k.path}
2037	        </ListItem>

2039	
2040	      {(status || error) && (

2044	        </Box>

2048	        <Text dimColor>↑↓ navigate · a add key · d remove key · Esc back</Text>
2049	      </Box>
2050	    </Box>

2083	import { Setup } from "./screens/Setup.js"

2090	function AppShell(): React.ReactNode {

2093

2108	      {screen === "key-list" && <KeyList onBack={goBack} />}

2175	
2176	SSH key manager for Git providers.

2178	Usage:
2179	  sshx                            Open interactive TUI

2181	  sshx list                       List managed keys
2182	  sshx remove <key>               Remove a managed key

2188	
2189	Setup options:
2190	  -e, --email <email>     Email for the SSH key
2191	  -k, --key <name>        Key file name (default: id_<type>_<provider>)
2192	  -H, --host <host>       Custom SSH host alias
2193	  --force                 Overwrite existing key
2194	  --no-git-config         Skip per-host git config
2195	  --no-browser            Skip opening provider settings page
2196	  --no-clipboard          Skip copying public key to clipboard
2197	
2198	Providers: github, gitlab, bitbucket, azure
2199	`.trim() + "\n")
2200	}
2201	
2202	type Flag = {
2203	  email?: string
2204	  key?: string
2205	  host?: string
2206	  force: boolean
2207	  noGitConfig: boolean
2208	  noBrowser: boolean
2209	  noClipboard: boolean
2210	}
2211	
2212	function parseFlags(argv: string[]): { flags: Flag; rest: string[] } {
2213	  const flags: Flag = { force: false, noGitConfig: false, noBrowser: false, noClipboard: false }
2214	  const rest: string[] = []
2215	  for (let i = 0; i < argv.length; i++) {
2216	    const a = argv[i]!
2217	    if (a === "-e" || a === "--email") { flags.email = argv[++i]; continue }
2218	    if (a === "-k" || a === "--key") { flags.key = argv[++i]; continue }
2219	    if (a === "-H" || a === "--host") { flags.host = argv[++i]; continue }
2220	    if (a === "--force") { flags.force = true; continue }
2221	    if (a === "--no-git-config") { flags.noGitConfig = true; continue }
2222	    if (a === "--no-browser") { flags.noBrowser = true; continue }
2223	    if (a === "--no-clipboard") { flags.noClipboard = true; continue }
2224	    rest.push(a)
2225	  }
2226	  return { flags, rest }
2227	}
2228	
2229	async function main(): Promise<void> {
2230	  const argv = process.argv.slice(2)
2231	
2232	  if (argv.length === 0) {

2239	
2240	  if (command === "-h" || command === "--help" || command === "help") {
2241	    printHelp()

2246	    process.stdout.write(`sshx v${VERSION}\n`)

2248	  }
2249	
2250	  if (command === "list") {
2251	    const keys = await list()

2257	      })

2259	    return
2260	  }
2261	
2262	  if (command === "remove") {
2263	    const keyName = rest[0]

2266	      process.exit(1)

2270	    return
2271	  }
2272	
2273	  if (command === "copy") {
2274	    const keyName = rest[0]

2278	    }

2281	      process.stdout.write("Public key copied to clipboard.\n")
2282	    } else {
2283	      process.stdout.write(`${publicKey}\n`)
2284	    }
2285	    return
2286	  }
2287	
2288	  if (command === "test") {
2289	    const providerArg = rest[0]
2290	    if (!providerArg) {
2291	      process.stderr.write("Usage: sshx test <provider>\n")
2292	      process.exit(1)
2293	    }
2294	    const provider = normalizeProviderKey(providerArg)
2295	    if (!provider) {
2296	      process.stderr.write(`Unknown provider: ${providerArg}\n`)
2297	      process.exit(1)
2298	    }
2299	    const result = await testConnection(provider)
2300	    process.stdout.write(`${result.output}\n`)
2301	    process.exit(result.success ? 0 : 1)
2302	  }
2303	
2304	  if (command === "agent") {
2305	    const sub = rest[0]
2306	    if (sub === "list") {
2307	      const keys = await agentList()
2308	      if (keys.length === 0) {
2309	        process.stdout.write("No keys in agent.\n")
2310	      } else {
2311	        keys.forEach(k => process.stdout.write(`${k.fingerprint}  ${k.path}  ${k.type}\n`))
2312	      }
2313	      return
2314	    }
2315	    if (sub === "add") {
2316	      const keyName = rest[1]
2317	      if (!keyName) { process.stderr.write("Usage: sshx agent add <key>\n"); process.exit(1) }
2318	      await agentAdd(keyName)
2319	      process.stdout.write(`Added ${keyName} to agent.\n`)
2320	      return
2321	    }

2324	      if (!keyName) { process.stderr.write("Usage: sshx agent remove <key>\n"); process.exit(1) }
2325	      await agentRemove(keyName)
2326	      process.stdout.write(`Removed ${keyName} from agent.\n`)
2327	      return
2328	    }
2329	    process.stderr.write("Usage: sshx agent <list|add|remove> [key]\n")

If `@vancityayush/tui` is not on npm (install fails), install from GitHub instead:
```bash
bun add github:vancityAyush/tui react
bun add -d @types/react
```

3. Replace `package.json` with this exact content:
```json
{
  "name": "@vancityayush/sshx",
  "version": "0.4.0",
  "description": "Cross-platform SSH key manager for Git providers, implemented in TypeScript",
  "license": "MIT",
  "repository": {
    "type": "git",
    "url": "git+https://github.com/vancityAyush/sshx.git"
  },
  "homepage": "https://github.com/vancityAyush/sshx#readme",
  "bugs": {
    "url": "https://github.com/vancityAyush/sshx/issues"
  },
  "packageManager": "bun@1.3.10",
  "bin": {
    "sshx": "bin/sshx"
  },
  "scripts": {
    "build": "bun build ./src/cli.ts --target=node --format=cjs --outfile ./dist/cli.js",
    "typecheck": "bunx tsc --noEmit --project tsconfig.json",
    "test": "bun test"
  },
  "files": [
    "bin/",
    "dist/",
    "README.md",
    "scripts/ssh.sh",
    "scripts/ssh.ps1"
  ],
  "engines": {
    "node": ">=18"
  },
  "keywords": [
    "ssh", "git", "github", "gitlab", "bitbucket", "azure-devops", "cli", "typescript"
  ],
  "dependencies": {
    "@vancityayush/tui": "*",
    "react": "^18"
  },
  "devDependencies": {
    "@types/node": "^24.0.0",
    "@types/react": "^18",
    "typescript": "^5.9.3"
  }
}
```

4. Replace `tsconfig.json` with this exact content:
```json
{
  "compilerOptions": {
    "target": "ES2022",
    "module": "ESNext",
    "moduleResolution": "Bundler",
    "jsx": "react-jsx",
    "strict": true,
    "esModuleInterop": true,
    "forceConsistentCasingInFileNames": true,
    "skipLibCheck": true,
    "resolveJsonModule": true,
    "outDir": "dist",
    "types": ["node"]
  },
  "include": ["src/**/*.ts", "src/**/*.tsx"]
}
```

5. Run `bun install` to confirm no errors.

6. Commit:
```bash
git add package.json tsconfig.json bun.lock
git commit -m "chore: scaffold TUI rebuild — update deps and tsconfig for JSX"
```

**Important notes:**
- All commands run from `/Users/vancityayush/Development/vancityAyush/sshx/.worktrees/tui-rebuild`
- If @types/react install fails, that's OK — proceed without it
- After deleting src/, the empty directories still need to exist: `src/commands`, `src/ssh`, `src/tui/screens`, `src/tui/components`
- The package.json `dependencies` block will be updated by bun add, so the versions may differ — that's fine

When done, report:
- STATUS: DONE
- What was installed (exact package names/versions)
- Whether bun install succeeded
- The git commit SHA

file.content:
# sshx TUI Redesign

**Date:** 2026-04-22
**Status:** Approved

## Overview

Full rebuild of `sshx` from scratch as a TUI-first CLI. Discards all existing TypeScript source. Retains project goal: manage SSH keys on the local system for Git providers (GitHub, GitLab, Bitbucket, Azure DevOps).

Uses `@vancityayush/tui` (React/ink-based terminal UI library) for interactive mode. Flag-based invocation still works for scripting and CI.

---

## UX Model

**Hybrid:** no args → launches TUI; known command + args → runs headlessly and exits.

```
$ sshx                          # opens TUI
$ sshx setup github -e a@b.com  # headless, no TUI
$ sshx list                     # prints list, exits
$ sshx remove mykey             # removes key, exits
```

---

## Commands

| Command  | Description |
|----------|-------------|
| `setup`  | Generate SSH key, write SSH config, copy pubkey to clipboard, open provider settings URL |
| `list`   | Show all sshx-managed keys from `~/.ssh/config` |
| `remove` | Delete key files and SSH config entry |
| `test`   | Run `ssh -T` against a provider host |
| `agent`  | List/add/remove keys from ssh-agent |
| `copy`   | Copy a key's public key to clipboard |

---

## File Structure

```
sshx/
├── src/
│   ├── cli.ts                  # entry: parse args → headless command or TUI
│   ├── providers.ts            # PROVIDERS map (github/gitlab/bitbucket/azure)
│   ├── commands/
│   │   ├── setup.ts
│   │   ├── list.ts
│   │   ├── remove.ts
│   │   ├── test.ts
│   │   ├── agent.ts
│   │   └── copy.ts
│   ├── ssh/
│   │   ├── config.ts           # read/write/parse ~/.ssh/config
│   │   └── keygen.ts           # shell out to ssh-keygen
│   ├── platform.ts             # clipboard, browser-open, OS detection
│   └── tui/
│       ├── App.tsx             # screen router
│       ├── screens/
│       │   ├── MainMenu.tsx
│       │   ├── Setup.tsx
│       │   ├── KeyList.tsx
│       │   ├── TestConnection.tsx
│       │   └── Agent.tsx
│       └── components/
│           └── Header.tsx
├── package.json
└── tsconfig.json
```

**Constraint:** Nothing in `src/commands/` or `src/ssh/` imports from `src/tui/`. The TUI layer is purely additive.

---

## Architecture

### Entry Point (`cli.ts`)

1. Parse `process.argv`.
2. If a known command name is present → call the matching command function → exit with its status code.
3. If no args (or `--tui`) → `render(<App />)` from `@vancityayush/tui`.

### Command Functions

Each command is an `async function` that accepts typed options and returns a result object or throws a typed error. No UI dependencies. Used by both TUI screens and headless flag mode.

```ts
// example signature
export async function setup(opts: SetupOptions): Promise<SetupResult>
export async function list(): Promise<ManagedKey[]>
export async function remove(keyName: string): Promise<void>
```

### TUI Navigation

`App.tsx` holds `currentScreen` state (union type). `MainMenu` renders a `CustomSelect` list. Selecting an item sets screen. Each screen accepts an `onBack` prop; pressing Esc/`q` calls it → returns to `MainMenu`.

### Setup Wizard (4 steps, within Setup screen)

1. **Provider** — `CustomSelect`: GitHub, GitLab, Bitbucket, Azure DevOps
2. **Email** — `TextInput`, pre-filled from `git config user.email`
3. **Key name** — `TextInput`, pre-filled as `id_ed25519_<provider>`
4. **Generate** — `Spinner` while running `setup()`, then show pubkey + "Copied to clipboard" + "Opening browser..."

### KeyList Screen

`OrderedList` of managed keys. Each row: key name + provider label + copy/remove actions. Remove shows a `Dialog` confirmation before calling `remove()`.

### TestConnection Screen

Provider `CustomSelect` → call `test()` → stream `ssh -T` output into a `Text` block with a `Spinner` while running.

### Agent Screen

Show keys currently in ssh-agent. Buttons: add (select from managed keys list), remove from agent.

---

## Data Flow

### SSH Config Ownership

`src/ssh/config.ts` is the sole reader/writer of `~/.ssh/config`. It parses config into typed segments:

```ts
type ConfigSegment =
  | { kind: "text"; lines: string[] }
  | { kind: "host"; host: string; lines: string[]; managed: boolean }
```

`managed: true` when the block contains a `# sshx` comment. `list.ts` filters by this marker. No separate metadata file.

### Managed Key Tracking

sshx identifies its own SSH config blocks with a `# sshx` comment on the `Host` line. Example:

```
Host github.com  # sshx
  IdentityFile ~/.ssh/id_ed25519_github
  User git
```

### Auto-detect

`setup.ts` reads `git config user.email` and parses the current directory's remote URL to pre-fill email and guess provider before the wizard opens.

---

## Error Handling

### Setup Rollback

If `setup()` fails mid-way (e.g. key written but config write fails), it deletes partial artifacts before throwing. TUI shows error in red on the Setup screen; user can retry.

### Clipboard / Browser Failures

Non-fatal. `platform.ts` catches failures from `pbcopy`/`xclip`/`clip.exe` and `open`/`xdg-open`/`explorer.exe` and returns a warning string. TUI displays it but does not block completion.

### Flag-mode Errors

`cli.ts` catches thrown errors from command functions, prints message to stderr, exits with code 1. No TUI rendered.

---

## Providers

| Key        | Label         | Hostname                | Key Type  | Settings URL |
|------------|---------------|-------------------------|-----------|--------------|
| `github`   | GitHub        | `github.com`            | ed25519   | `https://github.com/settings/keys` |
| `gitlab`   | GitLab        | `gitlab.com`            | ed25519   | `https://gitlab.com/-/profile/keys` |
| `bitbucket`| Bitbucket     | `bitbucket.org`         | ed25519   | `https://bitbucket.org/account/settings/ssh-keys/` |
| `azure`    | Azure DevOps  | `ssh.dev.azure.com`     | rsa       | `https://dev.azure.com/_usersSettings/keys` |

---

## Runtime & Tooling

- **Runtime:** Bun (required by `@vancityayush/tui`)
- **Language:** TypeScript with JSX (`tsconfig` target: `node18`, `jsx: react`)
- **Build:** `bun build ./src/cli.ts --target=node --format=cjs --outfile ./dist/cli.js`
- **Type-check:** `bunx tsc --noEmit`
- **Dependencies:** `@vancityayush/tui`, `react`
- **Dev dependencies:** `@types/node`, `typescript`, `@types/react`

---
